title: Aeonium dodrantale
intro: Aeonium dodrantale, also known as dodrantale, is a succulent plant species native to the Canary Islands. It is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Aeonium dodrantale include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Aeonium dodrantale is used in herbal medicine to prepare remedies such as teas and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Aeonium dodrantale is classified within the family Crassulaceae. This succulent plant is a part of the genus Aeonium, which comprises over 60 species of evergreen succulents. Aeonium dodrantale is native to the Canary Islands and other parts of the Atlantic Macaronesia region. It is a shrubby perennial plant with rosettes of fleshy, silvery-green leaves. The classification of Aeonium dodrantale is further supported by its unique morphological and anatomical characteristics.
